steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The opening dependent clause “In assessing the films of Japanese director Akira Kurosawa,” introduces context and must connect to an independent clause. The verb “have missed” requires a subject; the blank must provide that subject and any preceding verbs.

steps.title: Apply the rule of parallel structure
steps.content: The rule is parallel structure: when a coordinating conjunction joins two verb phrases with the same subject, they should maintain parallel form with the subject and auxiliary in the first clause carrying to the second. Option A correctly applies this by providing one subject with two parallel predicates: “critics have focused on Western literary sources but have missed...” with “have” shared across both verbs. Options B, C, and D disrupt parallelism—B shifts the subject to “Kurosawa's use of Western literary sources,” C introduces redundancy by naming then pronouncing the same agent, and D separates the parallel ideas into independent clauses. That matches choice A — A is correct.
